title: Comparing metrics between vectormetrics and landscapemetrics R packages
text: vectormetrics package calculates landscape metrics based vector polygon representations. shares many metrics landscapemetrics package offers landscape metrics raster data. principle, packages produce similar nearly identical results long input data equivalent (e.g., raster vector representations exact polygon geometry). However, differences can arise due several factors, including computational approaches, treatment polygon boundaries, underlying assumptions landscape features measured. comparison uses landscapemetrics::landscape dataset, 30×30 cell raster 1m resolution containing 3 classes across 28 patches (9 patches class 1, 13 class 2, 6 class 3). landscape converted vector polygons comparison. Note: converting polygon data raster analysis landscapemetrics, rasterization process affects actual landscape structure. Coarser resolutions can eliminate small patches entirely, merge adjacent patches, distort patch boundaries – fundamentally changing measured. structural changes cascade dependent metrics: patch counts change, patch areas shapes shift, affects perimeter-based metrics (te, ed, shape, circle, lsi), distance calculations (enn, proximity), core area detection (cai, dcore). vignette uses idealized scenario exactly landscape formats show pure representation difference – practice, converting polygons raster change landscape structure thus metrics get.

title: Comparing metrics between vectormetrics and landscapemetrics R packages
text: comparison evaluates 78 metrics exist packages, organized across three analysis levels: Patch level: 9 metrics Class level: 39 metrics Landscape level: 30 metrics Beyond shared metrics, vectormetrics offers 61 exclusive metrics found landscapemetrics – primarily specialized shape complexity geometry measures suited vector polygon analysis.

what: Metrics with perfect or near-perfect agreement
title: Comparing metrics between vectormetrics and landscapemetrics R packages
text: Many metrics show perfect near-perfect agreement, absolute differences less 0.001 test landscape. metrics robust vector-raster representation difference rely count-based, proportion-based, compositional calculations behave consistently across representations. patches agree patch level, agreements propagate class landscape levels since higher levels aggregate patch-level data. Patch-level metrics close agreement: 4 unique metrics 4 patch-level metrics show close agreement across patches within class 1. foundational patch-level agreements carried forward higher aggregation levels. Note p_core idential use core distance threshold – practice, differences core area detection methods (buffer vs. erosion) can lead differences core area metrics. Class-level metrics close agreement: 17 unique metrics (class 1 shown) 17 class-level metrics show close agreement (class 1 shown). Class-level agreement reflects patch-level patterns, since class metrics aggregate across patches within class. Landscape-level metrics close agreement (abs_diff < 0.001): 21 metrics 21 landscape-level metrics differences 0.001 show near-perfect agreement. Landscape-level patterns reflect patch class agreements feed .
code: patch_close <- patch_metrics |>   filter(abs_diff < 0.001, class == 1) |>   group_by(metric_name) |>   ungroup() |>   select(metric_name, class, n_patches_vm, n_patches_lsm, value_vm, value_lsm, abs_diff, vm_units) |>   arrange(metric_name)  kable(   patch_close,   digits = 3,   caption = sprintf("Patch-level metrics with close agreement: %d unique metrics",                      n_distinct(patch_close$metric_name)),   col.names = c("Metric", "Class", "N Patches VM", "N Patches LSM", "VM Value", "LSM Value", "Abs Diff", "Units") ) class_close <- class_metrics |>   filter(abs_diff < 0.001, class == 1) |>   group_by(metric_name) |>   ungroup() |>   select(metric_name, class, value_vm, value_lsm, abs_diff, vm_units) |>   arrange(metric_name)  kable(   class_close,   digits = 3,   caption = sprintf("Class-level metrics with close agreement: %d unique metrics (class 1 shown)",                      n_distinct(class_close$metric_name)),   col.names = c("Metric", "Class", "VM Value", "LSM Value", "Abs Diff", "Units") ) landscape_close <- landscape_metrics |>   filter(abs_diff < 0.001) |>   select(metric_name, value_vm, value_lsm, abs_diff, vm_units) |>   distinct(metric_name, .keep_all = TRUE) |>   arrange(metric_name)  kable(   landscape_close,   digits = 3,   caption = sprintf("Landscape-level metrics with close agreement (abs_diff < 0.001): %d metrics",                      nrow(landscape_close)),   col.names = c("Metric", "VM Value", "LSM Value", "Abs Diff", "Units") )

title: Comparing metrics between vectormetrics and landscapemetrics R packages
text: metrics differ substantially packages due fundamental differences vector raster data represent landscape features. Patch-level metrics notable differences Class-level metrics notable differences Landscape-level metrics notable differences main reasons metric differences across representation types (differ): Vector: Naturally include landscape boundary part polygon geometry (default behavior) Raster: Typically exclude boundary edges calculations Affected metrics: te, ed, shape, shape_mn, shape_sd, circle, circle_mn, circle_sd, circle_cv, lsi Vector (CIRCLE metrics): Standardize smallest circumscribing circle Raster: Standardize circumscribing square Affected metrics: circle, circle_mn, circle_sd, circle_cv Vector: Contracts patch boundaries inward specified distance (map units) Raster: Excludes edge cells erosion filtering (cells) Affected metrics: ncore, cai, cai_mn, cai_sd, cai_cv, tca, dcore_mn, dcore_sd, dcore_cv, dcad, ndca, cpland, core_cv Vector: Measures exact polygon boundaries (continuous geometry) Raster: Measures cell centers patch edges (discrete, grid-aligned points) Affected metrics: enn, enn_mn, enn_sd, enn_cv
code: patch_diffs <- patch_metrics |>   filter(abs_diff > 0.001) |>   arrange(desc(abs_diff)) |>   group_by(metric_name) |>   slice(1) |>   ungroup() |>   select(metric_name, class, value_vm, value_lsm, abs_diff, vm_units, diff_explanation) |>   arrange(metric_name)  kable(   patch_diffs,   digits = 3,   caption = "Patch-level metrics with notable differences",   col.names = c("Metric", "Class", "VM Value", "LSM Value", "Abs Diff", "Units", "Explanation") ) class_diffs <- class_metrics |>   filter(abs_diff > 0.001) |>   arrange(desc(abs_diff)) |>   group_by(metric_name) |>   slice(1) |>   ungroup() |>   select(metric_name, class, value_vm, value_lsm, abs_diff, vm_units, diff_explanation) |>   arrange(metric_name)  kable(   class_diffs,   digits = 3,   caption = "Class-level metrics with notable differences",   col.names = c("Metric", "Class", "VM Value", "LSM Value", "Abs Diff", "Units", "Explanation") ) landscape_diffs <- landscape_metrics |>   filter(abs_diff > 0.001) |>   select(metric_name, value_vm, value_lsm, abs_diff, vm_units, diff_explanation) |>   distinct(metric_name, .keep_all = TRUE) |>   arrange(metric_name)  kable(   landscape_diffs,   digits = 3,   caption = "Landscape-level metrics with notable differences",   col.names = c("Metric", "VM Value", "LSM Value", "Abs Diff", "Units", "Explanation") )

code: # Load vectormetrics metrics reference vm_metrics <- vectormetrics::vm_metrics  # metric_map already loaded in setup chunk above # Extract metrics that exist only in vectormetrics (lsm_fun is NA) exclusive_metrics <- metric_map |>   filter(is.na(lsm_fun)) |>   select(metric_name, level, vm_fun) |>   distinct() |>   left_join(vm_metrics |> select(function_name, type, description), by = c("vm_fun" = "function_name")) |>   select(metric_name, level, type, description) |>   mutate(level = factor(level, levels = c("patch", "class", "landscape"))) |>   arrange(metric_name)  kable(   exclusive_metrics,   caption = sprintf("All %d vectormetrics-exclusive metrics", nrow(exclusive_metrics)),   col.names = c("Metric", "Level", "Type", "Description") )

text: vectormetrics R package calculating landscape shape metrics vector layers. aim provide set metrics can characterize landscape patterns properties shapes defined polygons multipolygons. title: Landscape Metrics for Categorical Map Patterns in Vector Data
text: functions vectormetrics start vm_ (vector metrics). second part name specifies level (patch - p, class - c landscape - l). last part function name abbreviation corresponding metric (e.g. enn euclidean nearest-neighbor distance rect rectangularity). landscape class level functions also suffix end, specifies aggregation method (e.g. mean, sd).
code: # Patch level ## vm_p_"metric" vm_p_area() vm_p_square()  # Class level ## vm_c_"metric"[_"aggregation"] vm_c_np() vm_c_shape_sd()  # Landscape level ## vm_l_"metric"[_"aggregation"] vm_l_lpi() vm_l_square_mn()

title: Landscape Metrics for Categorical Map Patterns in Vector Data
text: now two utility functions available package. First one get_polygon_patches() breaks multipolygon geometries polygons. two types neighborhood relations available: 4 (edge) 8 (vertex). function enables users create set geometries aggregated shapes analyze shape’s properties separately.  Another utility function get_axes() calculates length major minor axes shape. used calculate elongation metric vm_p_elong() since length axes might useful information , get_axes() exported separate function.

title: Calculate multiple vectormetrics at once — calculate_vm
text: landscape sf object polygon multipolygon geometry. level Optional. Character vector levels include. One `"patch"`, `"class"`, `"landscape"`. `NULL` (default), levels returned based arguments. metric Optional. Character vector metric abbreviations include (e.g., `"area"`, `"enn"`). `NULL` (default), metrics returned based arguments. type Optional. Character vector metric types include (e.g., `"shape metric"`, `"area edge metric"`). `NULL` (default), types returned based arguments. Optional. Character vector function names use (e.g., `c("vm_p_area", "vm_c_ca")`). specified, overrides `level`, `metric`, `type` arguments. class_col Optional. name class column landscape. Required class-level metrics. patch_col Optional. name patch ID column. `NULL`, patch IDs generated automatically. verbose Logical. Print warning messages. Default: `TRUE`. ... Additional arguments passed metric functions (e.g., `edge_depth = 0.8` core area metrics).

title: Calculate multiple vectormetrics at once — calculate_vm
text: tibble columns: `level`, `class`, `id`, `metric`, `value`.

title: Calculate multiple vectormetrics at once — calculate_vm
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Calculate patch-level metrics # Note: edge_depth is needed for core area metrics (cai, core, ncore) calculate_vm(vector_patches, level = "patch", edge_depth = 0.8)  # Calculate specific metrics by abbreviation (no additional arguments needed) calculate_vm(vector_patches, metric = c("area", "perim"))  # Calculate specific functions calculate_vm(vector_patches, what = c("vm_p_area", "vm_p_perim"))  # Calculate class-level metrics (requires class_col) calculate_vm(vector_landscape, what = "vm_c_ca", class_col = "class")  # Calculate landscape-level metrics (some require class_col and edge_depth) calculate_vm(vector_landscape, level = "landscape", class_col = "class",              edge_depth = 0.8, class_max = 10) } # }
